title: potentilla lanata var. asturica
intro: Potentilla lanata var. asturica, also known as the downy cinquefoil, is a plant species within the rose family (Rosaceae) native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potent medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Potentilla lanata var. asturica include its potential to support the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and can be consumed as a tea or taken as a supplement to reap its medicinal benefits.
